input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user migrated from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP) and is getting exit code 8 when test assemblies produce zero tests. This is a known behavioral difference — MTP returns exit code 8 when no tests are discovered, whereas VSTest treated that as success.

Let me look at their project files to understand the current setup, and also invoke the relevant skill for MTP migration.

 ConditionalTests.cs
 TestProject.csproj

data.content:
This is a known MTP behavioral difference. **Exit code 8 means "no tests discovered"** — MTP treats zero-test assemblies as an error by default, unlike VSTest which returned success.

The fix is a single property in your project file:

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Do not fail with exit code 8 when no tests are discovered (e.g. conditional compilation). -->
    <TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>

data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the issue clearly. The test project uses `EnableMSTestRunner` (Microsoft.Testing.Platform) and has conditional compilation (`#if INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS`). When `INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS` is not defined, the assembly contains zero tests, and MTP returns exit code 8 ("no tests found"), which causes CI to fail.

The fix is to set the MSBuild property `<TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>` in the project file. This tells MTP to return exit code 0 instead of 8 when no tests are discovered.


I'll just apply the fix directly rather than checking for migration tools.

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Do not fail with exit code 8 when no tests are discovered (e.g. conditional compilation). -->
    <TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>

data.content:
**Summary:** Added `<TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>` to the project file.

- **Exit code 8** is MTP's "no tests discovered" signal — VSTest had no equivalent.
- Setting this property to `false` makes MTP return exit code 0 when zero tests are found, matching the old VSTest behavior.
- If you have multiple test projects, you can put this property in a shared `Directory.Build.props` instead.
